channel.title: NPR Topics: Home Page Top Stories
channel.description: Top stories in the U.S. and world news, politics, health, science, business, music, arts and culture. Nonprofit journalism with a mission. This is NPR.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

image.title: NPR Topics: Home Page Top Stories

item.title: What to know about US military strikes on alleged drug boats
item.description: The U.S. military strikes on alleged drug boats have killed over 200 people. The attacks began in September and aim to stem drug flow, but critics question their legality and effectiveness.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. military strikes on alleged drug boats have killed over 200 people. The attacks began in September and aim to stem drug flow, but critics question their legality and effectiveness.

(Image credit: Jacquelyn Martin)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Russian attack on Ukraine kills at least 11 and traps others in damaged buildings
item.description: Russia attacked Ukraine overnight Tuesday, killing at least 11 people and trapping others, authorities said. Residential buildings and other civilian infrastructure were damaged in eight of Kyiv's districts.
item.content:encoded:
Russia attacked Ukraine overnight Tuesday, killing at least 11 people and trapping others, authorities said. Residential buildings and other civilian infrastructure were damaged in eight of Kyiv's districts.

(Image credit: Efrem Lukatsky)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Iran halts talks with U.S. over Israeli actions in Lebanon, Gaza
item.description: Trumps says Israel and Hezbollah will stop fighting after Iran stops talks with U.S. over Israeli operations in southern Lebanon, Gaza
item.content:encoded:
Trumps says Israel and Hezbollah will stop fighting after Iran stops talks with U.S. over Israeli operations in southern Lebanon, Gaza

(Image credit: STR/AP Photo/undefined)
item.dc:creator: Hadeel Al-Shalchi

item.title: Justice Department says it will abide by court order pausing its 'anti-weaponization' fund
item.description: The Justice Department said it 'strongly disagrees' with the court's ruling that paused a $1.776 fund for victims of government "weaponization," but would still abide by it.
item.content:encoded:
The Justice Department said it 'strongly disagrees' with the court's ruling that paused a $1.776 fund for victims of government "weaponization," but would still abide by it.

(Image credit: Win McNamee)
item.dc:creator: Ryan Lucas

item.title: Florida sues OpenAI and Sam Altman over alleged safety lapses
item.description: The lawsuit accuses the company of failing to warn users that ChatGPT could be dangerous and instead marketing it as safe and reliable.
item.content:encoded:
The lawsuit accuses the company of failing to warn users that ChatGPT could be dangerous and instead marketing it as safe and reliable.

(Image credit: Justin Sullivan)
item.dc:creator: Shannon Bond

item.title: AI giant Anthropic prepares to sell stock to the public; files preliminary IPO paperwork
item.description: The Anthropic IPO, and those of other AI-related firms like OpenAI, could be among the biggest in U.S. history.
item.content:encoded:
The Anthropic IPO, and those of other AI-related firms like OpenAI, could be among the biggest in U.S. history.

(Image credit: RICCARDO MILANI/Hans Lucas/AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: John Ruwitch

item.title: Serena Williams returns to tennis, announcing 'next chapter'
item.description: A tennis legend at 44, Serena Williams is returning to pro tennis this month, announcing plans to play in the HSBC Championships in London. Williams, a mother of two, retired nearly four years ago.
item.content:encoded:
A tennis legend at 44, Serena Williams is returning to pro tennis this month, announcing plans to play in the HSBC Championships in London. Williams, a mother of two, retired nearly four years ago.

(Image credit: Angela Weiss)
item.dc:creator: Bill Chappell

item.title: Richard Pryor's daughter studies the N-word — a word he used, then disavowed
item.description: Historian Elizabeth Stordeur Pryor spent years researching the racial slur, but never revealed that her father was the legendary comic who used it profusely. Her new book is Something We Said.
item.content:encoded:
Historian Elizabeth Stordeur Pryor spent years researching the racial slur, but never revealed that her father was the legendary comic who used it profusely. Her new book is Something We Said.

(Image credit: Lennox McLendon)
item.dc:creator: Tonya Mosley

item.title: Remote work -- not AI -- has sidelined recent college graduates, research finds
item.description: Research from the New York Fed finds that younger college graduates have been sidelined by remote work in recent years, as companies may be reluctant to hire those needing more training and mentoring.
item.content:encoded:
Research from the New York Fed finds that younger college graduates have been sidelined by remote work in recent years, as companies may be reluctant to hire those needing more training and mentoring.

(Image credit: Maksym Belchenko)
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu

item.title: A cancer vaccine made just for you. mRNA is back and it's fighting melanoma
item.description: A study finds that an mRNA vaccine is highly effective at preventing recurrence of this dangerous skin cancer, when used in combination with Keytruda, an immunotherapy drug.
item.content:encoded: A study finds that an mRNA vaccine is highly effective at preventing recurrence of this dangerous skin cancer, when used in combination with Keytruda, an immunotherapy drug.
item.dc:creator: Allison Aubrey
